sources.label: OpenNav waypoint database (VAMPI, MEKAR, NILAM, GOLUD, PUKAR, IDKUT)
mh370wiki.net: History of the flight (quotes Factual Information 2015)
mh370wiki.net: Waypoints and MH370 (filed flight plan table from FI 2015)
Gilbert, 'An Analysis of MH370's Flight Path Between Waypoint IGARI...' (PDF; quotes FI 2015 pages 1-2, 7, 46, 54 event times)
Iannello (radiantphysics) / mh370search: 18:22 last primary radar position interpretations

meta.method: waypoint/report-quoted positions; NOT a figure digitization; positions between anchors are along airway/great-circle interpolation between published anchors
notes: Times are UTC on 7 March 2014 (MYT = UTC+8, i.e. local 8 March). Event times are quoted from the Malaysian Factual Information report (2015) as cited by mh370wiki.net and the Gilbert PDF timeline (which cites FI page numbers).

Epoch 1 lateral path is modeled as departure runway 32R -> filed waypoints PIBOS, IKUKO -> IGARI (filed-route coordinates from the FI flight plan table on mh370wiki.net). Aircraft was cleared 'direct IGARI' at 16:43; filed waypoints lie within a few NM of the direct track, so the polyline is representative, not radar-derived.

The exact FI-quoted coordinates of the 17:21:13 last secondary-radar return could not be verified from any reliable fetched source (a widely circulated value ~6°55'N 103°34.7'E appears only on unreliable aggregators). The 17:21:13 point here is dead-reckoned 42 s past IGARI on the 059° track toward BITOD (FI: FMS right turn to 059° toward BITOD had begun) and is marked interpolated.

The 18:22:12 last primary radar fix has two published interpretations ~117 km apart: (a) Safety Investigation Report figure: 10 NM beyond MEKAR on N571 toward NILAM = 6.577655N 96.340864E (used here); (b) Beijing Lido military slide: 200 NM from Butterworth bearing 295T = 6.871123N 97.354047E (not used).

OpenNav MEKAR (6.5039N 96.4911E) and NILAM (6.7564N 95.9764E) were cross-validated: the independently published '10 NM past MEKAR toward NILAM' position 6.577655N 96.340864E lies exactly 10 NM along the MEKAR->NILAM leg built from the OpenNav coordinates.

Epoch 2 positions between the FI-timed anchors (turnback, Kota Bharu coast 17:37:22, 6 NM south of Penang 17:52:35, Pulau Perak 18:02, VAMPI 18:13:00, north of MEKAR 18:21:50, last fix 18:22:12) are straight-line interpolations; the military radar track between anchors was published only as report figures, which were not digitized.

Implied ground speeds between some Epoch 2 anchors are schematic; FI notes radar-derived ground speeds varied. The turnback 'hook' shape (right turn toward BITOD reversing into a left turn to ~231-240°) follows the FI description as quoted by mh370wiki.net and the Gilbert PDF.

Altitudes: 35,000 ft from FI ACARS 17:07:48 report; 35,700 ft at 17:30:35-17:35 and 29,500 ft at 18:22 are military-radar-derived values quoted by Wikipedia from official reports. Interpolated points carry altFt null.

epoch1.label: Cleared to push back, gate C1 WMKK (airport reference point)
epoch1.source: time: FI p1 via Gilbert PDF; position: OurAirports WMKK

epoch1.label: Takeoff from runway 32R (threshold coordinates, field elev ~70 ft)
epoch1.source: time: FI p1 via Gilbert PDF; position: OurAirports runways.csv WMKK 32R threshold

epoch1.label: Climbing right turn to the northeast (cleared FL180 direct IGARI at 16:43)
Near filed waypoint PIBOS, climbing (cleared FL350 at 16:50)
epoch1.source: position: FI filed flight plan (PIBOS N0320.5 E10203.1) via mh370wiki.net; time interpolated

epoch1.label: Crew reports maintaining FL350
epoch1.source: time/alt: FI App 1.18E via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated

epoch1.label: Last ACARS transmission (HDG 027, FL350, Mach 0.821)
epoch1.source: time/alt/hdg: FI p46 via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated

epoch1.label: Near filed waypoint IKUKO
epoch1.source: position: FI filed flight plan (IKUKO N0545.2 E10313.4) via mh370wiki.net; time interpolated

epoch1.label: Final voice transmission: 'Good night, Malaysian three seven zero'
epoch1.source: time: FI App 1.18E via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated

epoch1.label: Passing waypoint IGARI (6°56'12"N 103°35'06"E)
epoch1.source: time: FI p2 via Gilbert PDF; position: Wikipedia MH370 article, cross-checked with FI flight plan table (N0656.2 E10335.1) via mh370wiki.net

epoch1.label: Last secondary radar return; Mode S symbol dropped 17:20:36, position symbol disappeared 17:21:13
epoch1.source: time: FI p2 via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated (dead-reckoned ~42 s past IGARI on 059° track toward BITOD; exact FI coordinates not verifiable from fetched sources)

epoch2.label: Start of primary-radar-only epoch (same point as last secondary return)
Brief continuation toward BITOD; apex of right turn before turnback (FI: turn back began between IGARI and BITOD)
Left turn through north - turnback hook
Established southwest-bound (~231-240°) back toward the Malay peninsula
Military radar: 35,700 ft on 231° magnetic heading (17:30:35-17:35)
epoch2.source: time/alt/hdg: Wikipedia MH370 article (from official reports); position interpolated

epoch2.label: Crossing coast north of Kota Bharu (near waypoint GOLUD 6.285N 102.2775E / WMKC)
epoch2.source: time: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF (mh370search gives 17:36:43); position interpolated on coast north of Kota Bharu, anchored by OpenNav GOLUD and OurAirports WMKC

epoch2.label: Crossing the Malay peninsula toward Penang
6 NM south of Penang Island (track aligned toward KENDI, SW of Penang)
epoch2.source: time/description: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated 6 NM south of Penang Island south coast (island position from OurAirports WMKP)

epoch2.label: Right turn onto ~290° up the Strait of Malacca
Passing Pulau Perak (island at 5°40'50"N 98°56'27"E)
epoch2.source: time: Wikipedia MH370 article (from FI); position: Wikipedia Pulau Perak island coordinates (aircraft passed near the island)

epoch2.label: Strait of Malacca, toward airway N571 at VAMPI
Passing waypoint VAMPI on N571
epoch2.source: time: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF; position: OpenNav VAMPI (6°10'56"N 97°35'08"E)

epoch2.label: Along N571 VAMPI-MEKAR (FI: track ran slightly north of the airway)
Passing north of waypoint MEKAR (MEKAR at 6.5039N 96.4911E)
epoch2.source: time: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated ~7 NM past MEKAR along N571 toward NILAM (MEKAR/NILAM from OpenNav)

epoch2.label: Last primary radar fix, 10 NM past MEKAR on N571 toward NILAM (SIR interpretation; Lido-slide alternative: 6.871123N 97.354047E)
epoch2.source: time: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF; position: Safety Investigation Report interpretation as computed by mh370search/radiantphysics (Iannello); alt: Wikipedia MH370 article
